metadata.title:
FEDERAL CONTRACTORS


CAUSE UNION TROUBL E

pages.raw_text:
According to the statement Of
Matthew Dow, head of the Seattle
contracting firm of Dow, & Co., the
whole trouble in that city with
building trades unions has been
caused by McGrath & Duhamel,
contractors on the federal building
there. They started the open shop
and 1t was forced on many con-
tractors who were willing to abide
by their union agreements. Dow
says his firm will keep faith with
the unions.


This situation will appeal to Spo-
kane laboring men, as they have
made unsuccessful efforts to have
the contractor on the Spokane fed
eral building employ local labor un-
der guarantee to protect him from
delay by strikes or dissension.


From this it would appear that
the federal buildings erected out
of the peoples money are really
designed as monuments to the
policy of contractors who prefer to
employ the floating element rather
than the solid taxpaying citizen of
the communities in which the
buildings are erected. They mean
merely that so many non-citizens
were given the work of building
them, while the citizens found work
at some other occupation in order
to raise money to help pay the con-
tractor his profit on the work.
